events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate sequence check for rigid & PVC conduit commercial project. Observed: wrong glue/no primer on PVC joints. Work in-progress. Need decision fail because non-compliant. Risk? PVC joints without primer/wrong glue can lead to joint failure, leaks, separation, critical maybe high. In commercial electrical conduit, not pressurized fluid but structural integrity of raceway. NEC Art 352 requires PVC conduit to be installed per manufacturer instructions and approved fittings; solvent cement and primer per listing. No primer/wrong glue = installed defective, non-compliant. Work stage in-progress. percent_complete maybe 40? value 0. sound_source etc default empty string/0. order? Sequence violation: before continuing, joints must be prepared, primer applied, correct solvent cement, proper cure, inspection. Need list order step-ids maybe ["prepare-joints", "apply-primer", "apply-approved-solvent-cement", "assemble-and-hold", "cure", "inspect"]. workable maybe empty or activities that can still start unaffected? The task asks state what must happen before work can continue. Use actions. findings. references NEC Art. 352.10? Actually Art 352 covers RTRC and PVC. Need include Art. 344 maybe for rigid. But scenario only PVC issue. Could mention NEC Art. 352 (PVC conduit installation), 110.3(B) installation per listing/instructions. Also manufacturer instructions. Let's craft JSON. Ensure valid JSON, no extra.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 45,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [
    "cut-and-deburr-pvc",
    "clean-joint-surfaces",
    "apply-approved-primer",
    "apply-listed-solvent-cement",
    "assemble-and-hold-joint",
    "allow-proper-cure-time",
    "visual-inspection-of-joints"
  ],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "PVC conduit joints assembled without required primer",
    "Incorrect/unspecified glue used on PVC conduit joints",
    "Joints are not installed per the conduit listing and manufacturer instructions",
    "Existing joints must be remade or verified before concealment or energization"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop PVC conduit jointing work immediately",
    "Remove or cut out defective joints",
    "Re-make joints using manufacturer-approved primer and listed solvent cement for the conduit type",
    "Follow cure times and assembly instructions from the manufacturer",
    "Re-inspect joints before backfill, concealment, or wire pull"
  ],
  "rationale": "PVC conduit joints require a listed solvent-cement system and primer per the manufacturer's installation instructions. Using the wrong glue or omitting primer creates a non-compliant, mechanically weak joint that can separate and violates NEC Article 352. Because the work is actively in progress and the defect affects installed conduit, the sequence must be corrected before continuing.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 352 (PVC Conduit)",
    "NEC 110.3(B) - Installation and Use per Listing/Instructions"
  ]
}
